I guess I worry about how my friend might react—what if they don’t respond the way I hope, or what if they don’t have the time to talk? That uncertainty makes it hard to even start. Plus, there’s this nagging voice in my head that tells me I shouldn’t burden them with my feelings, like maybe I’m just overreacting or being dramatic. It’s tough to shake that off. And then, even if I do manage to send the message, I start thinking about what I’ll actually say when we do talk. It feels like there’s so much pressure to get it right, and I don’t want to mess it up. So, I guess it’s that mix of fear and pressure that makes it all feel a bit daunting.
